compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Progress (session 0122D9): ADR 0029 prerequisite LANDED (commit c84cd2241) — inbound field diff now suppresses the archived/deleted→Done round-trip so a locally-archived ticket is not flipped to 'closed' by Jira's Done echo (5 RED tests green, _TERMINAL_LOCAL_STATUSES in lock-step with classify). REMAINING (444d core): the level-triggered binding-store walk that (a) reads archived-bound tickets' true state via a targeted read (archived tickets are dropped from the 'rebar list --full' snapshot — Layer 1) and (b) emits the TERMINAL_TRANSITION Decision as an archived/deleted→Done outbound status mutation through the existing apply_outbound/dispatch. This is unified with 13eb's PROBE_GET on the SAME walk. It constructs real production-Jira transitions, so per the epic it should land behind 7d23's circuit-breaker + shadow rollout. Detection is already live via 8de5's classifier consumer (bridge-fsck flags these 5 as would_terminal).

compiled_state.description:
## Summary
When a synced local ticket is archived or moved to status `deleted`, its bound Jira issue is left live (e.g. "To Do") indefinitely. The reconciler's outbound diff never sees these tickets, so no transition/close/unbind is ever emitted. Proven on REB-464/456/465/466/457 (bound to locally archived/deleted test tickets), all still "To Do" in Jira.

## Root Cause (two filter layers, an unintended gap)
- Layer 1 (archived): the reconciler builds its local snapshot via `rebar list --full` (`reconcile.py:455`) with no `--include-archived`; `reducer/_api.py:234` drops `archived=True`. Archived tickets never enter the diff.
- Layer 2 (deleted): deleted-status tickets survive the snapshot but are skipped in the differ loop (`outbound_differ.py:537-540`, `excluded_statuses={archived,deleted}`).
- Smoking gun: `outbound_fields.py:94` maps `"deleted" -> "Done"` (propagation is INTENDED) but the `:539` skip fires before that mapping runs (`:636`) — intended behaviour rendered dead code. `OutboundMutation` declares a `"delete"` action (`outbound_differ.py:245`) but nothing ever constructs one. Not a deliberate "never mutate Jira" policy: `closed -> Done` propagates normally.
- Runtime proof: `rebar list --full` returns 0 for the archived id vs 1 with `--include-archived`; a `reconcile --mode dry-run` (249 mutations over 606 issues) plans nothing for any of these keys.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Sync a local ticket to Jira (binding confirmed).
2. Archive it, or transition it to `deleted` locally.
3. Run `rebar reconcile --mode dry-run`.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: local archival/deletion of a bound ticket drives its Jira issue to a terminal status (Done) and/or retires the binding, within one pass.
- Actual: no mutation is planned; the Jira issue is stranded live forever.

## Proposed design (perf-safe, binding-driven)
Drive terminal-transition detection from the BINDING STORE, not a full archived-ticket scan, so cost scales with the number of *synced* tickets (~606), never with the total number of archived tickets:
- Each pass compute `missing = bindings.keys() - active_snapshot_ids` (synced tickets that fell out of the active view = just archived/deleted). Cheap set-diff over ~606 bindings.
- For only those ids, do a targeted state read and emit the already-wired terminal transition (`deleted`/`archived -> Done`) via `_LOCAL_TO_JIRA_STATUS` + `_diff_fields`.
- Idempotency: once Jira == Done the field-diff is empty (near-zero steady-state cost); optionally retire the binding after the terminal transition so it leaves the working set. Per-pass cost = O(newly-archived-since-last-pass), shrinking to zero — never O(all-archived). Do NOT switch the full snapshot to `--include-archived`.
- Unifies with the class-C binding GC (same binding-store iteration).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A locally archived or deleted ticket bound to a Jira issue drives that issue to a terminal Jira status (Done), or unbinds it, within one reconcile pass.
- [ ] Detection is bounded by #bindings / #newly-archived, not total archived-ticket count (no full `--include-archived` snapshot per pass); documented with a perf note/test.
- [ ] The dead `_LOCAL_TO_JIRA_STATUS["deleted"]="Done"` mapping becomes reachable (or is replaced by an explicit terminal-transition path).
- [ ] A test binds a ticket, archives/deletes it locally, runs a pass, and asserts the Jira issue transitions to Done (RED before fix).
- [ ] The concrete drift (REB-464/456/465/466/457) reconciles after the fix.

## Notes
Discovered comparing Jira REB vs the local store. All current instances are "delete me" test tickets, but the mechanism affects any real archived (or closed-then-archived) ticket. `bridge_fsck` does not detect this (see the class-D ticket). Files: `outbound_differ.py`, `outbound_fields.py`, `reconcile.py`, `reducer/_api.py`.

## Guardrails (must-preserve — prior-art + archaeology; folds in the corrected plan)
Approved policy: local archive/delete drives the Jira issue to a terminal status (Done). But:
- [ ] Drive the terminal transition from the per-binding BASELINE + binding-store walk, NOT from snapshot membership; act only on a CONFIRMED terminal/404 (grace-counted), never on snapshot-absence (L13/L16/L17).
- [ ] Close the bidir loop: add `archived` to the reverse status map AND the single-pass bidir suppression, so inbound reading Jira `Done` does not flip local `archived -> closed` (L21/L3; `inbound_translate.py:121`). Today `archived` maps nowhere on the reverse path.
- [ ] Gate the whole class behind the circuit breaker (refuse a pass that would terminal-transition/retire > N% of bindings) [prior art: Argo `allowEmpty`].
- [ ] Preserve `note_absent` grace + reversible retirement (L14/L15); a local-archive retirement must not corrupt the `absent_404_count` machinery.
- [ ] Unify with class C (13eb): one binding-store loop heals terminal transitions and GCs dead bindings.
- [ ] Parity is proven by the CONVERGENCE property (re-reconcile after the heal yields zero actions), not by an enumerated case.

## Implementation contract (foundation-first — read before coding)
DEPENDS ON amiss-bun-wince (baf7): do NOT hand-roll a detection path. This bug is the `TERMINAL_TRANSITION` Decision cell of `classify()` (src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/classify.py) plus its apply handler.
1. Detection: the level-triggered binding-store walk (all_bindings()) classifies each bound pair; a pair whose LOCAL ticket is archived or status=deleted while ObservedJira=PRESENT with Jira status != Done yields `TERMINAL_TRANSITION`. Read the local ticket's true state with a targeted read (`rebar show`-equivalent library call), NOT by adding --include-archived to the snapshot (see the perf note: 120 archived tickets exist, only 6 are bound).
2. Apply: emit the already-mapped transition via `_LOCAL_TO_JIRA_STATUS` ("deleted"->"Done"; add "archived"->"Done") through the existing outbound mutation dispatch (typed_dispatch/apply_outbound) — construct a normal status-transition mutation; do NOT invent a new mutation type.
3. ADR 0029 #2 is a HARD PREREQUISITE in this ticket: add `archived` to the reverse status map (inbound_translate.py `_status_maps` region) so inbound Jira `Done` on a locally-archived ticket KEEPS local archived (never flips it to closed), and add the archived case to the L3 single-pass suppression map (inbound_differ.py). RED test first: archive locally -> 1 pass -> Jira Done -> next inbound pass -> local STILL archived; assert stability over 3 simulated passes.
4. Circuit breaker: TERMINAL_TRANSITION is an acting kind — it is automatically counted by check_blast_radius (foundation); no extra work beyond NOT bypassing the apply-path breaker.
5. Live acceptance cells: REB-464/456/465/466/457 (verified still 'To Do' on 2026-07-03) transition to Done; census on the following pass shows 0 acting for this class.
Files: classify.py (cell), outbound_fields.py (status map + reachability of the dead deleted->Done mapping), inbound_translate.py + inbound_differ.py (reverse map + L3), apply_outbound.py/typed_dispatch.py (handler), tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/classify/ + diffing/.
compiled_state.title: Reconciler never propagates local archive/delete to the bound Jira issue (stranded live issues)

verify_commands.dd_text: archived/deleted bound ticket -> Jira Done in one pass (RED first)

verify_commands.command: rebar reconcile --mode dry-run  # plan must contain the 5 transitions; then bootstrap-strict heals
